items.title: High Court hears bid to overturn block on 22-year Mount Pleasant coal mine extension
items.summary: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.

items.title: Australian-born, London-based MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to oust Britsh PM Keir Starmer
items.summary: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

items.title: Victorian emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition
items.summary: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.

items.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species
items.summary: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.

items.title: Australia-UAE defence commitments do not involve offensive action against Iran, says government
items.summary: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.

items.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget
items.summary: The ABC has asked Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, about the latest federal budget.

items.title: Will CGT and negative gearing budget changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
items.summary: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

items.title: Blues On Broadbeach celebrates 25 years of music by the sea
items.summary: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

items.title: Jayson Gillham on legal battle with Melbourne Symphony Orchestra and his tour Keys to Life
items.summary: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

items.title: Labor bets the house on young Australians' desperation to buy their own
items.summary: If the reforms help younger Australians believe home ownership is within reach, Labor can expect to collect a political dividend.

items.title: Travel quiz, May 13: Find your passport, it's time for a whirlwind world trip
items.summary: Where was the world's first permanent commercial bungee jumping site set up? And which country is home to the historic city of Timbuktu?

items.title: Frontbenchers resign as more MPs call for Starmer to quit as UK PM
items.summary: Sir Keir Starmer is striking a defiant tone despite a growing number of his Labour colleagues urging him to step down.

items.title: There was more in the 2026 budget than we were expecting
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Treasurer concedes broken promise in budget carries 'political risk'
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Government says economic environment demands bold reform
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Labor unveils 'ambitious' plan for tax reset in 2026 budget
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: $250 tax break for millions of workers in federal budget 2026
items.summary: Labor will raise taxes on investment properties and some trust funds, giving the revenue back to workers with a new $250 tax break unveiled in the federal budget.

items.title: Why Chalmers reined in the property tax lurks in federal budget 2026
items.summary: One of the more striking elements from the now well-entrenched program of pre-budget leaks is the lack of outrage from the population or even the opposition.

items.title: Capital gains tax changes in federal budget could see investment shift from property to shares
items.summary: Investors in stocks will be affected by changes to capital gains tax, but experts say the budget is still likely to see some investment move away from property and into shares.

items.title: With this federal budget, Australia's last baby boomer PM becomes King of the Kids
items.summary: Tonight, Anthony Albanese becomes the first serving prime minister in decades to take a hammer to the most sacred shibboleth in Australian domestic politics.

items.title: A five-minute guide to what's in the federal budget to help with cost of living
items.summary: Fresh tax cuts, an instant $1,000 tax reduction and cheaper medicines: here's what the federal government's latest budget means for Australians.

items.title: Government breaks promise with restrictions to negative gearing and capital gains tax discount in federal budget
items.summary: Tax perks that property investors have used for decades to maximise profits and offset their losses will be restricted from tonight, in one of the most significant changes to the tax system in years.

items.title: Federal budget 2026: Winners and losers
items.summary: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors. So who wins and who misses out?

items.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court
items.summary: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

items.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in fraudulent property investment scam, court hears
items.summary: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

items.title: Izak Rankine says he has bounced back from suspension for homophobic slur
items.summary: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

items.title: Pakistan market blast kills at least nine people
items.summary: A blast in a market kills at least nine people as Islamabad blames Kabul for a weekend attack that has left 15 police officers dead.

items.title: Emotional Senate tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby amid debate on focus of NT inquiry
items.summary: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

items.title: Man charged with son's torture and murder previously offered to plead guilty to manslaughter, court hears
items.summary: Reinhardt Bosch has been charged with the murder and torture of his son Rhuan in 2022, and offered to plead guilty to the lesser charge of manslaughter, a court has heard.

items.title: Murderer sentenced to life in prison over death of 22yo Dale Aaron McKenna
items.summary: The court in Toowoomba heard Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe was motivated by revenge after discovering his on-again off-again girlfriend had started a relationship with the 22-year-old victim.

items.title: Sydney cage fighter found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing best friend
items.summary: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.

items.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
items.summary: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

items.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
items.summary: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

items.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
items.summary: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

items.title: Tax reform to help 75,000 buyers into first homes, government says ahead of federal budget's release
items.summary: In a political push to get ahead of criticism it has broken election promises, Labor is making a case for how wealth is taxed in Australia by promising the budget will open the housing market to younger buyers.

items.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
items.summary: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

items.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
items.summary: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

items.title: Research highlights antisemitism impact on women, royal commission told
items.summary: About 80 per cent of Jewish women in Australia have experienced antisemitism personally or against an immediate family member in the past two years, the royal commission has heard.

items.title: Canberra teen charged with planning terrorist attack and transmitting violent extremist material
items.summary: The 17-year-old appeared in the ACT Children's Court today charged with one count of planning or preparing for a terrorist act and one count of transmitting violent extremist material.

items.title: Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie uses parliamentary privilege to attack Labor MPs
items.summary: It comes after more than a week of scrutiny on ministerial lovers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm.

items.title: Brisbane's council backflips on promise to introduce licences for landlords using Airbnb, Stayz
items.summary: Brisbane City Council announces it will not go ahead with plans to regulate landlords' use of short-stay apps such as Airbnb and Stayz.

items.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi
items.summary: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

items.title: Four more Australian National University council members quit after Julie Bishop's resignation as chancellor
items.summary: The acting chancellor of the Australian National University confirms four more members of the university council have resigned, following last week's exit of former chancellor Julie Bishop.

items.title: Four ministers quit as UK PM Starmer tells cabinet he is staying put
items.summary: The British prime minister has defied calls to resign, telling ministers he will "get on with governing".

items.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'
items.summary: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.

items.title: Carlton boss says Voss resignation proof club is in 'genuine transition'
items.summary: Carlton CEO Graham Wright says the club will embark on a thorough process to determine the successor to Michael Voss.

items.title: What giving up alcohol taught me about myself
items.summary: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

items.title: Australian government finalising plans to bring hantavirus cruise ship passengers home
items.summary: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

items.title: Sitting near the Strait of Hormuz, seafarers are in limbo as Iran war drags on
items.summary: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.

items.title: Prosecutors drop domestic violence charges against Lockhart River mayor Wayne Butcher
items.summary: The long-serving mayor of Lockhart River on eastern Cape York was facing seven domestic violence charges but the case against him won't proceed.

items.title: Jessica Pegula putting family experience in NFL and NHL to use in tennis prize money protest
items.summary: Jessica Pegula is putting her family experience of the NFL and NHL to use in tennis players' fight against the French Open for increased prize money.

items.title: Alleged driver charged over deaths of two girls in Viveash crash
items.summary: Police charge a 24-year-old man with dangerous driving causing death, a fortnight after two girls died from the injuries they suffered when their car smashed into a tree in Viveash.

items.title: Darwin chemical recycler prepares to make diesel from gas condensate
items.summary: Global Resource Recovery NT says production of the first diesel made from Central Australian gas condensate will leave Darwin in July.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship's captain praises crew and guests
items.summary: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

items.title: Captain of Hantavirus-hit cruise ship praises crew and guests
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved
items.summary: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

items.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
items.summary: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

items.title: ASX slips as budget comes into focus and DroneShield faces ASIC probe — as it happened
items.summary: Australian shares slid while DroneShield was being investigated by ASIC over disclosures and share trading. Look back on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our live blog.

items.title: More than 70 UK Labour MPs call for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation — as it happened
items.summary: Six ministerial aides have stepped down and more than 70 UK Labour MPs have publicly called for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation.

items.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts
items.summary: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.

items.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
items.summary: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

items.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
items.summary: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

items.title: Alexei Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Jannik Sinner at Italian Open
items.summary: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

items.title: Apple's Tim Cook and Tesla's Elon Musk among top US CEOs to accompany Trump to China
items.summary: According to a list shared by a White House official, speaking on condition of anonymity, Elon Musk and Tim Cook will be accompanied by 15 other chief executive officers when Donald Trump visits China.
